Co-authored-by: bherila <ben+gh@herila.net>

feat: persist Copilot session IDs for resume across restarts

Uses the SDK's built-in resumeSession API. The Copilot CLI already
keeps session data on disk; this change stores the Discord key →
Copilot session ID mapping so sessions can be reconnected on startup.

Changes to src/copilot.ts:
- Add SessionStore class persisting { discordKey: sessionId } to
  ~/.config/ai-assistant/sessions.json
  - Atomic writes via tmp file + renameSync (no partial-write corruption)
  - Skip-if-unchanged to avoid redundant disk writes on normal resume
- getOrCreateSession: tries resumeSession() first; falls back to
  createSession on failure (expired/deleted); always updates store
  with the active session ID
- Pending/.then() guards: checks this.pending.get(key) === creation
  before committing to sessions/store — prevents zombie sessions
  when /reset races with an in-flight creation
- resetSession: calls client.deleteSession() for permanent removal
  (vs disconnect which only frees memory); also removes from store
  so /reset truly wipes and prevents future resume
- shutdown: unchanged — disconnect() preserves on disk for resume

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Priority 6: remove redundant screenX > 255 check in RenderBGTile() (+6.1%)

Caller already guarantees ppu_cycles_x < 256, so screenX can never
exceed 255. Removing the dead branch allows JIT to better optimize
the inner loop (unrolling/branch elimination).

204.10 -> 216.45 FPS (+6.1%, far exceeded expected <1%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: use D3 data joins for all render layers

Replace imperative for-loop DOM creation with D3 .data().join()
enter/update/exit pattern across all four render layers:

- Layer 1 (tree links): data join on LayoutLink[]
- Layer 2 (IC containers + IC nodes): data join on LayoutICContainer[] and LayoutNode[]
- Layer 3 (advisor links + advisor nodes): data join on LayoutLink[] and LayoutNode[]
- Layer 4 (manager nodes): data join on LayoutNode[] with key fn (d => d.id)

Since the full DOM clear (selectAll('*').remove()) runs before each render,
only the enter selection fires today. This establishes the idiomatic D3
pattern required for future incremental/differential rendering where the
clear can be removed and update/exit selections become active.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Introduce SpecCompliance enum for supporting lenient mode (#463)

* Introduce SpecCompliance enum for supporting lenient mode

* fix: allow unknown XML attributes per 3MF spec anyAttribute

The 3MF spec uses xs:anyAttribute on all XML element definitions,
meaning any attribute from any namespace is valid. The previous
validate_attributes() function incorrectly rejected unknown attributes,
causing files from BambuStudio/OrcaSlicer (which add a 'printable'
attribute on <item>) to fail parsing even in lenient mode.

Fixes #462

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Priority 9: CPU opcode dispatch Action[256] table (+7.6%)

- Replace switch(opcode) with pre-populated Action[256] opHandlers array
- InitOpHandlers() assigns lambda for each of 256 opcode entries
- Fall-through groups share same Action reference (NOP, HLT, DOP, etc.)
- Result: 189.75 -> 204.10 avg FPS (+7.6%)
- Debug JIT does not fully optimize dense switch to jump table;
  delegate array provides consistent O(1) dispatch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update src/main/java/frc/robot/commands/IntakeWhileShooting.java

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: make /model, /reset, /history thread-context-aware

When invoked inside a bot-owned thread (i.e. a /chat thread session),
use interaction.channelId as the session key instead of user.id so
the commands operate on the correct session.

- /model set: switches model for the thread session; success message
  says 'for this thread' vs 'for your session' based on context
- /reset: clears the thread session; message says 'This thread's
  Copilot session has been reset' when invoked inside a thread
- /history: shows exchanges from the thread session rather than
  the user's personal session

All three now use the same key resolution pattern as bot.ts
MessageCreate (isThread() ? channelId : user.id).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update src/main/java/frc/robot/commands/ShootCommand.java

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(security): implement hardening features 1.5, 1.6, 1.7

Feature 1.5 — Shell injection prevention + auth hardening:
- Add sanitize_git_ref() to executor.py (regex + shlex.quote)
- Apply in bot.py cmd_diff and slack.py _cmd_diff; reject invalid refs
- Require SLACK_CHANNEL_ID in _validate_config() when PLATFORM=slack
- Tests: TestSanitizeGitRef (11), TestCmdDiffSanitization (4), test_slack_missing_channel_id_raises (1)

Feature 1.6 — Prompt injection hardening:
- Frame summarize_if_long() prompt with <OUTPUT> tags + do-not-follow instruction
- Frame build_context() history with <HISTORY> tags + do-not-follow instruction
- Validate SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE path in factory.py (must not be inside REPO_DIR)
- Log warning when COPILOT_SKILLS_DIRS entries are inside REPO_DIR
- Frame voice transcription in bot.py and slack.py before _run_ai_pipeline()
- Tests: summarize framing (1), history framing (3+existing updated), SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE inside-repo raises (1)

Feature 1.7 — Secret redaction:
- Add allow_secrets: bool = False to BotConfig
- Create src/redact.py: SecretRedactor with 12 patterns + value-based redaction + redact_git_commit_cmd()
- Apply redaction in bot.py: _stream_to_telegram, _run_ai_pipeline non-streaming, all shell output handlers
- Apply redaction in slack.py: _reply, _send, _edit (all outgoing messages)
- Pass SecretRedactor to run_shell() at all 8 call sites
- Install commit-msg hook at startup: excludes test files, respects ALLOW_SECRETS=true, min token length 36
- Tests: TestRedact* (14 tests)

All tests: 413/417 passing (4 pre-existing container env var failures, unchanged)
Lint: clean

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: per-subscription push visibility filtering

Previously, push notifications used a global visibility  if ANYcheck
connected client tab was visible, no push was sent to any device. This
meant a visible desktop tab suppressed phone notifications.

Now each push subscription endpoint is associated with its client IDs.
sendToAll skips only endpoints that have at least one visible client.
Endpoints with no visible client (or no connected client) receive push
as expected.

- Add registerClient(clientId, endpoint) to PushService
- Add isEndpointVisible() for per-endpoint visibility check
- Move visibility filtering from maybeNotify into sendToAll
- Frontend sends clientId on /api/push/subscribe
- New /api/push/register-client endpoint for SSE reconnect
- Frontend auto-registers push endpoint on each SSE connect

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: /chat spawns Discord thread for isolated context

- /chat in a guild channel creates a public thread (24h auto-archive)
  from the deferred reply; session keyed by thread.id
- /chat inside an existing thread reuses the thread (avoids nested
  thread API error)
- DMs fall back to inline reply with user.id as session key
- Thread name sanitized (newlines stripped)
- MessageCreate handler detects bot-owned threads (ownerId === bot uid)
  and routes all messages to handleMention with channelId as session key
- handleMention gains optional sessionKey param (defaults to user.id)
- SessionManager.sendMessage uses per-session promise queue to serialize
  concurrent messages and prevent state corruption
- resetSession also clears the message queue

commit.message:
Fix CI: build audio inference at preview.2 to match csproj refs

Pre-existing audio model packages (SileroVad, SpeechT5, WhisperBase/Tiny,
ASTAudioSet, CLAP) reference MLNet.AudioInference.Onnx >= 0.1.0-preview.2,
so the local build must use that version.

Priority 8: RenderBGTile optimize bgColor pre-compute + merge duplicate condition

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ol-superset): exclude Superset-calculated columns from dbt YAML checks

Superset datasets can define 'calculated columns' — columns with a
non-empty `expression` field that are computed by Superset at query time
from other columns (e.g. `substring(order_created_on, 1, 4)`). These are
not warehouse columns and will never appear in a dbt model YAML, so
flagging them as 'not documented in dbt model YAML' produces false-positive
warnings.

Changes:
- DatasetAsset gets a new `calculated_columns: set[str]` field populated
  by `_load_datasets()`. Columns with a non-empty `expression` go there;
  plain warehouse columns go into `columns` as before.
- Validation 4a (dataset columns vs dbt YAML) now only iterates
  `dataset.columns`, skipping `calculated_columns` entirely.
- Validation 4c (chart column refs vs dataset) now resolves against
  `dataset.columns | dataset.calculated_columns`, because charts may
  legitimately reference calculated columns by name.
- Tests updated: fixture gains a calculated column; two new assertions
  verify the plain/calculated split. Total: 28 tests, all pass.

Result: 69 → 59 warnings (10 fewer false positives).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

covers Blue Smart IP22/IP65, Phoenix Smart IP43, and other Victron
AC charger models.

commit.message:
Fix CI: build all dependency packages from source + add NuGet source mapping

Three issues prevented CI from publishing:
1. local-packages dir didn't exist in CI
2. MLNet.ImageInference.Onnx came from a separate repo (mlnet-image-inference-custom-transforms)
3. GitHub Packages feed had breaking 0.2.0-preview.1 superseding 0.1.0-preview.1

Fix: Build text inference, image inference, and ModelPackages from source
into local-packages. Add packageSourceMapping to nuget.config to force
MLNet.* and ModelPackages to resolve from local-packages only.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add /status and /history slash commands

/status: calls client.getAuthStatus() + getStatus() (with idempotent
start()) and displays auth user, type, host, and CLI version.

/history [count]: retrieves session.getMessages() for the calling user,
filters to user.message and assistant.message events (skipping sub-agent
turns via parentToolCallId), and shows the last N exchanges (default 5,
max 20), truncated to Discord's 2000-char limit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ol-superset): skip chart column checks for virtual (SQL-defined) datasets

Virtual datasets are defined by a SQL query rather than a direct table
reference. Their YAML `columns` list is a cached Superset metadata
snapshot that may not enumerate every column the SQL produces, so checking
chart column refs against it generates false-positive warnings.

Changes:
- [3/4] print an info line showing how many virtual datasets are skipped
  and why, so operators can see they are accounted for
- [3/4] success message now says 'simple Trino dataset(s)' and counts only
  non-virtual datasets to give an accurate picture
- [4/4] 4c (chart → dataset column check) now skips virtual datasets;
  their column surface is validated via SQL table-ref checks in 4b instead
- [4a/4b] comments clarified to explain the virtual/simple distinction

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix CI: also build inference packages from source

MLNet.ImageInference.Onnx 0.2.0-preview.1 on the GitHub Packages feed has
breaking API changes vs 0.1.0-preview.1. Build inference packages from the
mlnet-text-inference-custom-transforms source (matching upstream CI pattern)
to get the correct 0.1.0-preview.1 versions in local-packages.

commit.message:
Fix CI: create local-packages dir in upstream clone before pack

The upstream model-packages-prototype nuget.config also references a
local-packages source. Need to mkdir it in the shallow clone before
dotnet pack can restore and build ModelPackages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Priority 1: remove PollInterrupts() empty stub calls (+78% FPS)

- Removed ~214 PollInterrupts() call sites from CPU.cs instruction dispatch
- Function was a confirmed empty stub; JIT overhead was substantial
- Result: 187.70 -> 334.75 avg FPS (+78.3%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Forms: Create synced form when inserting form variations via block inserter (#47335)

* Forms: Create synced form when inserting form variations via block inserter

When users insert a form variation directly from the block inserter (e.g.,
"Contact Form"), WordPress creates the block with innerBlocks immediately,
bypassing the VariationPicker. This meant synced forms were not being created.

This adds a new hook `useCreateSyncedFormOnInsertion` that detects when a form
block is inserted with inner blocks but without a ref attribute, and
automatically creates a synced form when central form management is enabled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Forms: Use variationName attribute and wasBlockJustInserted for synced form creation

Simplify the useCreateSyncedFormOnInsertion hook by:
- Using wasBlockJustInserted selector to detect newly inserted blocks
- Using variationName attribute directly instead of deriving variation from inner blocks
- Removing complex heuristic-based variation detection logic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Forms: Address review feedback for synced form creation hook

- Add entity preloading before setting ref to prevent skeleton flash
- Replace unstable useEffect deps with empty array and ref guard
- Remove unnecessary registry.batch() wrapper around single dispatch
- Update getVariationTitle docblock to accurately describe return cases
- Replace flaky setTimeout in tests with deterministic microtask flushing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Forms: Simplify synced form creation hook for performance

- Short-circuit useSelect after creation attempt to stop unnecessary store queries
- Collapse guard clauses into single shouldCreate boolean
- Match variations by name instead of attributes.variationName
- Add variationName to variations missing it so name-based lookup works
- Simplify tests by removing redundant mock data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Forms: Fix TypeScript error for currentPostId type mismatch

getCurrentPostId() returns string | number, but createSyncedForm
expects number. Wrap with Number() to satisfy the type checker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use variationName in insertion logic

Only create a synced form on insertion if the block has a variationName: add a truthy attributes.variationName check to the shouldCreate condition and include it in the dependency array. Also fix the variation title lookup to compare against variations' attributes.variationName (not v.name) so the correct form title is selected.

* Prevent post-unmount updates in form insertion hook

Add cancellation handling and safer entity preload in useCreateSyncedFormOnInsertion: introduce a cancelled flag with a cleanup return to avoid setting attributes or showing notices after unmount, wrap getEntityRecord in try/catch as a best-effort preload, and bail early when cancelled. Update tests to cover preload failure (still sets ref), adjust variationName test data, ensure no synced form is created when variationName is missing, and add a test for unknown variationName fallback behavior.

* Forms: Add useEffect dependencies to fix synced form creation for variations

The empty dependency array meant the effect only ran on mount. If
shouldCreate was false initially (e.g. innerBlocks not yet populated),
the synced form would never be created. The hasAttemptedCreation ref
prevents double execution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Reset creation flag on cleanup and silence deps

Reset hasAttemptedCreation.current to false in the effect cleanup so the creation attempt flag is cleared when the component is unmounted. The effect's dependency array was replaced with an empty array and an eslint-disable comment was added to suppress react-hooks/exhaustive-deps; this makes the effect run only once on mount (intentional to avoid repeated creation attempts). Previously the effect listed shouldCreate, attributes, innerBlocks, currentPostId, setAttributes, createSuccessNotice, and createErrorNotice as dependencies.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Priority 11: managed array to unsafe pointer (TRI_SEQ, DUTYLOOKUP, secondaryOAM, corruptOamRow)

- APU.cs: TRI_SEQ[32] readonly int[] -> int* (Marshal.AllocHGlobal, initAPU init)
- APU.cs: DUTYLOOKUP[4,8] int[,] -> int* (flattened, index duty*8+seq)
- PPU.cs: secondaryOAM byte[] -> byte*, corruptOamRow bool[] -> byte*
- Main.cs: allocate + zero-init secondaryOAM and corruptOamRow in init()
- Result: 181.70 -> 187.70 avg FPS (+3.3%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
